{"content": "     1|# Developing
     2|
     3|This project can run fully in local dev without setting up PostgreSQL manually.
     4|
     5|## Deployment Modes
     6|
     7|For mode definitions and intended CLI behavior, see `doc/DEPLOYMENT-MODES.md`.
     8|
     9|Current implementation status:
    10|
    11|- canonical model: `local_trusted` and `authenticated` (with `private/public` exposure)
    12|
    13|## Prerequisites
    14|
    15|- Node.js 20+
    16|- pnpm 9+
    17|
    18|## Dependency Lockfile Policy
    19|
    20|GitHub Actions owns `pnpm-lock.yaml`.
    21|
    22|- Do not commit `pnpm-lock.yaml` in pull requests.
    23|- Pull request CI validates dependency resolution when manifests change.
    24|- Pushes to `master` regenerate `pnpm-lock.yaml` with `pnpm install --lockfile-only --no-frozen-lockfile`, commit it back if needed, and then run verification with `--frozen-lockfile`.
    25|
    26|## Start Dev
    27|
    28|From repo root:
    29|
    30|```sh
    31|pnpm install
    32|pnpm dev
    33|```
    34|
    35|This starts:
    36|
    37|- API server: `http://localhost:3100`
    38|- UI: served by the API server in dev middleware mode (same origin as API)
    39|
    40|`pnpm dev` runs the server in watch mode and restarts on changes from workspace packages (including adapter packages). Use `pnpm dev:once` to run without file watching.
    41|
    42|`pnpm dev:once` auto-applies pending local migrations by default before starting the dev server.
    43|
    44|`pnpm dev` and `pnpm dev:once` are now idempotent for the current repo and instance: if the matching Paperclip dev runner is already alive, Paperclip reports the existing process instead of starting a duplicate.
    45|
    46|Inspect or stop the current repo's managed dev runner:
    47|
    48|```sh
    49|pnpm dev:list
    50|pnpm dev:stop
    51|```
    52|
    53|`pnpm dev:once` now tracks backend-relevant file changes and pending migrations. When the current boot is stale, the board UI shows a `Restart required` banner. You can also enable guarded auto-restart in `Instance Settings > Experimental`, which waits for queued/running local agent runs to finish before restarting the dev server.
    54|
    55|Tailscale/private-auth dev mode:
    56|
    57|```sh
    58|pnpm dev --bind lan
    59|```
    60|
    61|This runs dev as `authenticated/private` with a private-network bind preset.
    62|
    63|For Tailscale-only reachability on a detected tailnet address:
    64|
    65|```sh
    66|pnpm dev --bind tailnet
    67|```
    68|
    69|Legacy aliases still map to the old broad private-network behavior:
    70|
    71|```sh
    72|pnpm dev --tailscale-auth
    73|pnpm dev --authenticated-private
    74|```
    75|
    76|Allow additional private hostnames (for example custom Tailscale hostnames):
    77|
    78|```sh
    79|pnpm paperclipai allowed-hostname dotta-macbook-pro
    80|```
    81|
    82|## One-Command Local Run
    83|
    84|For a first-time local install, you can bootstrap and run in one command:
    85|
    86|```sh
    87|pnpm paperclipai run
    88|```
    89|
    90|`paperclipai run` does:
    91|
    92|1. auto-onboard if config is missing
    93|2. `paperclipai doctor` with repair enabled
    94|3. starts the server when checks pass
    95|
    96|## Docker Quickstart (No local Node install)
    97|
    98|Build and run Paperclip in Docker:
    99|
   100|```sh
   101|docker build -t paperclip-local .
   102|docker run --name paperclip \
   103|  -p 3100:3100 \
   104|  -e HOST=0.0.0.0 \
   105|  -e PAPERCLIP_HOME=/paperclip \
   106|  -v "$(pwd)/data/docker-paperclip:/paperclip" \
   107|  paperclip-local
   108|```
   109|
   110|Or use Compose:
   111|
   112|```sh
   113|docker compose -f docker/docker-compose.quickstart.yml up --build
   114|```
   115|
   116|See `doc/DOCKER.md` for API key wiring (`OPENAI_API_KEY` / `ANTHROPIC_API_KEY`) and persistence details.
   117|
   118|## Docker For Untrusted PR Review
   119|
   120|For a separate review-oriented container that keeps `codex`/`claude` login state in Docker volumes and checks out PRs into an isolated scratch workspace, see `doc/UNTRUSTED-PR-REVIEW.md`.
   121|
   122|## Database in Dev (Auto-Handled)
   123|
   124|For local development, leave `DATABASE_URL` unset.
   125|The server will automatically use embedded PostgreSQL and persist data at:
   126|
   127|- `~/.paperclip/instances/default/db`
   128|
   129|Override home and instance:
   130|
   131|```sh
   132|PAPERCLIP_HOME=/custom/path PAPERCLIP_INSTANCE_ID=dev pnpm paperclipai run
   133|```
   134|
   135|No Docker or external database is required for this mode.
   136|
   137|## Storage in Dev (Auto-Handled)
   138|
   139|For local development, the default storage provider is `local_disk`, which persists uploaded images/attachments at:
   140|
   141|- `~/.paperclip/instances/default/data/storage`
   142|
   143|Configure storage provider/settings:
   144|
   145|```sh
   146|pnpm paperclipai configure --section storage
   147|```
   148|
   149|## Default Agent Workspaces
   150|
   151|When a local agent run has no resolved project/session workspace, Paperclip falls back to an agent home workspace under the instance root:
   152|
   153|- `~/.paperclip/instances/default/workspaces/<agent-id>`
   154|
   155|This path honors `PAPERCLIP_HOME` and `PAPERCLIP_INSTANCE_ID` in non-default setups.
   156|
   157|For `codex_local`, Paperclip also manages a per-company Codex home under the instance root and seeds it from the shared Codex login/config home (`$CODEX_HOME` or `~/.codex`):
   158|
   159|- `~/.paperclip/instances/default/companies/<company-id>/codex-home`
   160|
   161|If the `codex` CLI is not installed or not on `PATH`, `codex_local` agent runs fail at execution time with a clear adapter error. Quota polling uses a short-lived `codex app-server` subprocess: when `codex` cannot be spawned, that provider reports `ok: false` in aggregated quota results and the API server keeps running (it must not exit on a missing binary).
   162|
   163|## Worktree-local Instances
   164|
   165|When developing from multiple git worktrees, do not point two Paperclip servers at the same embedded PostgreSQL data directory.
   166|
   167|Instead, create a repo-local Paperclip config plus an isolated instance for the worktree:
   168|
   169|```sh
   170|paperclipai worktree init
   171|# or create the git worktree and initialize it in one step:
   172|pnpm paperclipai worktree:make paperclip-pr-432
   173|```
   174|
   175|This command:
   176|
   177|- writes repo-local files at `.paperclip/config.json` and `.paperclip/.env`
   178|- creates an isolated instance under `~/.paperclip-worktrees/instances/<worktree-id>/`
   179|- when run inside a linked git worktree, mirrors the effective git hooks into that worktree's private git dir
   180|- picks a free app port and embedded PostgreSQL port
   181|- by default seeds the isolated DB in `minimal` mode from the current effective Paperclip instance/config (repo-local worktree config when present, otherwise the default instance) via a logical SQL snapshot
   182|
   183|Seed modes:
   184|
   185|- `minimal` keeps core app state like companies, projects, issues, comments, approvals, and auth state, preserves schema for all tables, but omits row data from heavy operational history such as heartbeat runs, wake requests, activity logs, runtime services, and agent session state
   186|- `full` makes a full logical clone of the source instance
   187|- `--no-seed` creates an empty isolated instance
   188|
   189|After `worktree init`, both the server and the CLI auto-load the repo-local `.paperclip/.env` when run inside that worktree, so normal commands like `pnpm dev`, `paperclipai doctor`, and `paperclipai db:backup` stay scoped to the worktree instance.
   190|
   191|`pnpm dev` now fails fast in a linked git worktree when `.paperclip/.env` is missing, instead of silently booting against the default instance/port. If that happens, run `paperclipai worktree init` in the worktree first.
   192|
   193|Provisioned git worktrees also pause seeded routines that still have enabled schedule triggers in the isolated worktree database by default. This prevents copied daily/cron routines from firing unexpectedly inside the new workspace instance during development without disabling webhook/API-only routines.
   194|
   195|That repo-local env also sets:
   196|
   197|- `PAPERCLIP_IN_WORKTREE=true`
   198|- `PAPERCLIP_WORKTREE_NAME=<worktree-name>`
   199|- `PAPERCLIP_WORKTREE_COLOR=<hex-color>`
   200|
   201|The server/UI use those values for worktree-specific branding such as the top banner and dynamically colored favicon.
   202|
   203|Print shell exports explicitly when needed:
   204|
   205|```sh
   206|paperclipai worktree env
   207|# or:
   208|eval "$(paperclipai worktree env)"
   209|```
   210|
   211|### Worktree CLI Reference
   212|
   213|**`pnpm paperclipai worktree init [options]`** — Create repo-local config/env and an isolated instance for the current worktree.
   214|
   215|| Option | Description |
   216||---|---|
   217|| `--name <name>` | Display name used to derive the instance id |
   218|| `--instance <id>` | Explicit isolated instance id |
   219|| `--home <path>` | Home root for worktree instances (default: `~/.paperclip-worktrees`) |
   220|| `--from-config <path>` | Source config.json to seed from |
   221|| `--from-data-dir <path>` | Source PAPERCLIP_HOME used when deriving the source config |
   222|| `--from-instance <id>` | Source instance id (default: `default`) |
   223|| `--server-port <port>` | Preferred server port |
   224|| `--db-port <port>` | Preferred embedded Postgres port |
   225|| `--seed-mode <mode>` | Seed profile: `minimal` or `full` (default: `minimal`) |
   226|| `--no-seed` | Skip database seeding from the source instance |
   227|| `--force` | Replace existing repo-local config and isolated instance data |
   228|
   229|Examples:
   230|
   231|```sh
   232|paperclipai worktree init --no-seed
   233|paperclipai worktree init --seed-mode full
   234|paperclipai worktree init --from-instance default
   235|paperclipai worktree init --from-data-dir ~/.paperclip
   236|paperclipai worktree init --force
   237|```
   238|
   239|Repair an already-created repo-managed worktree and reseed its isolated instance from the main default install:
   240|
   241|```sh
   242|cd ~/.paperclip/worktrees/PAP-884-ai-commits-component
   243|pnpm paperclipai worktree init --force --seed-mode minimal \
   244|  --name PAP-884-ai-commits-component \
   245|  --from-config ~/.paperclip/instances/default/config.json
   246|```
   247|
   248|That rewrites the worktree-local `.paperclip/config.json` + `.paperclip/.env`, recreates the isolated instance under `~/.paperclip-worktrees/instances/<worktree-id>/`, and preserves the git worktree contents themselves.
   249|
   250|For an already-created worktree where you want to keep the existing repo-local config/env and only overwrite the isolated database, use `worktree reseed` instead. Stop the target worktree's Paperclip server first so the command can replace the DB safely.
   251|
   252|**`pnpm paperclipai worktree reseed [options]`** — Re-seed an existing worktree-local instance from another Paperclip instance or worktree while preserving the target worktree's current config, ports, and instance identity.
   253|
   254|| Option | Description |
   255||---|---|
   256|| `--from <worktree>` | Source worktree path, directory name, branch name, or `current` |
   257|| `--to <worktree>` | Target worktree path, directory name, branch name, or `current` (defaults to `current`) |
   258|| `--from-config <path>` | Source config.json to seed from |
   259|| `--from-data-dir <path>` | Source `PAPERCLIP_HOME` used when deriving the source config |
   260|| `--from-instance <id>` | Source instance id when deriving the source config |
   261|| `--seed-mode <mode>` | Seed profile: `minimal` or `full` (default: `full`) |
   262|| `--yes` | Skip the destructive confirmation prompt |
   263|| `--allow-live-target` | Override the guard that requires the target worktree DB to be stopped first |
   264|
   265|Examples:
   266|
   267|```sh
   268|# From the main repo, reseed a worktree from the current default/master instance.
   269|cd /path/to/paperclip
   270|pnpm paperclipai worktree reseed \
   271|  --from current \
   272|  --to PAP-1132-assistant-ui-pap-1131-make-issues-comments-be-like-a-chat \
   273|  --seed-mode full \
   274|  --yes
   275|
   276|# From inside a worktree, reseed it from the default instance config.
   277|cd /path/to/paperclip/.paperclip/worktrees/PAP-1132-assistant-ui-pap-1131-make-issues-comments-be-like-a-chat
   278|pnpm paperclipai worktree reseed \
   279|  --from-instance default \
   280|  --seed-mode full
   281|```
   282|
   283|**`pnpm paperclipai worktree:make <name> [options]`** — Create `~/NAME` as a git worktree, then initialize an isolated Paperclip instance inside it. This combines `git worktree add` with `worktree init` in a single step.
   284|
   285|| Option | Description |
   286||---|---|
   287|| `--start-point <ref>` | Remote ref to base the new branch on (e.g. `origin/main`) |
   288|| `--instance <id>` | Explicit isolated instance id |
   289|| `--home <path>` | Home root for worktree instances (default: `~/.paperclip-worktrees`) |
   290|| `--from-config <path>` | Source config.json to seed from |
   291|| `--from-data-dir <path>` | Source PAPERCLIP_HOME used when deriving the source config |
   292|| `--from-instance <id>` | Source instance id (default: `default`) |
   293|| `--server-port <port>` | Preferred server port |
   294|| `--db-port <port>` | Preferred embedded Postgres port |
   295|| `--seed-mode <mode>` | Seed profile: `minimal` or `full` (default: `minimal`) |
   296|| `--no-seed` | Skip database seeding from the source instance |
   297|| `--force` | Replace existing repo-local config and isolated instance data |
   298|
   299|Examples:
   300|
   301|```sh
   302|pnpm paperclipai worktree:make paperclip-pr-432
   303|pnpm paperclipai worktree:make my-feature --start-point origin/main
   304|pnpm paperclipai worktree:make experiment --no-seed
   305|```
   306|
   307|**`pnpm paperclipai worktree env [options]`** — Print shell exports for the current worktree-local Paperclip instance.
   308|
   309|| Option | Description |
   310||---|---|
   311|| `-c, --config <path>` | Path to config file |
   312|| `--json` | Print JSON instead of shell exports |
   313|
   314|Examples:
   315|
   316|```sh
   317|pnpm paperclipai worktree env
   318|pnpm paperclipai worktree env --json
   319|eval "$(pnpm paperclipai worktree env)"
   320|```
   321|
   322|For project execution worktrees, Paperclip can also run a project-defined provision command after it creates or reuses an isolated git worktree. Configure this on the project's execution workspace policy (`workspaceStrategy.provisionCommand`). The command runs inside the derived worktree and receives `PAPERCLIP_WORKSPACE_*`, `PAPERCLIP_PROJECT_ID`, `PAPERCLIP_AGENT_ID`, and `PAPERCLIP_ISSUE_*` environment variables so each repo can bootstrap itself however it wants.
   323|
   324|## Quick Health Checks
   325|
   326|In another terminal:
   327|
   328|```sh
   329|curl http://localhost:3100/api/health
   330|curl http://localhost:3100/api/companies
   331|```
   332|
   333|Expected:
   334|
   335|- `/api/health` returns `{"status":"ok"}`
   336|- `/api/companies` returns a JSON array
   337|
   338|## Reset Local Dev Database
   339|
   340|To wipe local dev data and start fresh:
   341|
   342|```sh
   343|rm -rf ~/.paperclip/instances/default/db
   344|pnpm dev
   345|```
   346|
   347|## Optional: Use External Postgres
   348|
   349|If you set `DATABASE_URL`, the server will use that instead of embedded PostgreSQL.
   350|
   351|## Automatic DB Backups
   352|
   353|Paperclip can run automatic DB backups on a timer. Defaults:
   354|
   355|- enabled
   356|- every 60 minutes
   357|- retain 30 days
   358|- backup dir: `~/.paperclip/instances/default/data/backups`
   359|
   360|Configure these in:
   361|
   362|```sh
   363|pnpm paperclipai configure --section database
   364|```
   365|
   366|Run a one-off backup manually:
   367|
   368|```sh
   369|pnpm paperclipai db:backup
   370|# or:
   371|pnpm db:backup
   372|```
   373|
   374|Environment overrides:
   375|
   376|- `PAPERCLIP_DB_BACKUP_ENABLED=true|false`
   377|- `PAPERCLIP_DB_BACKUP_INTERVAL_MINUTES=<minutes>`
   378|- `PAPERCLIP_DB_BACKUP_RETENTION_DAYS=<days>`
   379|- `PAPERCLIP_DB_BACKUP_DIR=/absolute/or/~/path`
   380|
   381|## Secrets in Dev
   382|
   383|Agent env vars now support secret references. By default, secret values are stored with local encryption and only secret refs are persisted in agent config.
   384|
   385|- Default local key path: `~/.paperclip/instances/default/secrets/master.key`
   386|- Override key material directly: `PAPERCLIP_SECRETS_MASTER_KEY`
   387|- Override key file path: `PAPERCLIP_SECRETS_MASTER_KEY_FILE`
   388|
   389|Strict mode (recommended outside local trusted machines):
   390|
   391|```sh
   392|PAPERCLIP_SECRETS_STRICT_MODE=***
   393|```
   394|
   395|When strict mode is enabled, sensitive env keys (for example `*_API_KEY`, `*_TOKEN`, `*_SECRET`) must use secret references instead of inline plain values.
   396|
   397|CLI configuration support:
   398|
   399|- `pnpm paperclipai onboard` writes a default `secrets` config section (`local_encrypted`, strict mode off, key file path set) and creates a local key file when needed.
   400|- `pnpm paperclipai configure --section secrets` lets you update provider/strict mode/key path and creates the local key file when needed.
   401|- `pnpm paperclipai doctor` validates secrets adapter configuration and can create a missing local key file with `--repair`.
   402|
   403|Migration helper for existing inline env secrets:
   404|
   405|```sh
   406|pnpm secrets:migrate-inline-env         # dry run
   407|pnpm secrets:migrate-inline-env --apply # apply migration
   408|```
   409|
   410|## Company Deletion Toggle
   411|
   412|Company deletion is intended as a dev/debug capability and can be disabled at runtime:
   413|
   414|```sh
   415|PAPERCLIP_ENABLE_COMPANY_DELETION=false
   416|```
   417|
   418|Default behavior:
   419|
   420|- `local_trusted`: enabled
   421|- `authenticated`: disabled
   422|
   423|## CLI Client Operations
   424|
   425|Paperclip CLI now includes client-side control-plane commands in addition to setup commands.
   426|
   427|Quick examples:
   428|
   429|```sh
   430|pnpm paperclipai issue list --company-id <company-id>
   431|pnpm paperclipai issue create --company-id <company-id> --title "Investigate checkout conflict"
   432|pnpm paperclipai issue update <issue-id> --status in_progress --comment "Started triage"
   433|```
   434|
   435|Set defaults once with context profiles:
   436|
   437|```sh
   438|pnpm paperclipai context set --api-base http://localhost:3100 --company-id <company-id>
   439|```
   440|
   441|Then run commands without repeating flags:
   442|
   443|```sh
   444|pnpm paperclipai issue list
   445|pnpm paperclipai dashboard get
   446|```
   447|
   448|See full command reference in `doc/CLI.md`.
   449|
   450|## OpenClaw Invite Onboarding Endpoints
   451|
   452|Agent-oriented invite onboarding now exposes machine-readable API docs:
   453|
   454|- `GET /api/invites/:token` returns invite summary plus onboarding and skills index links.
   455|- `GET /api/invites/:token/onboarding` returns onboarding manifest details (registration endpoint, claim endpoint template, skill install hints).
   456|- `GET /api/invites/:token/onboarding.txt` returns a plain-text onboarding doc intended for both human operators and agents (llm.txt-style handoff), including optional inviter message and suggested network host candidates.
   457|- `GET /api/skills/index` lists available skill documents.
   458|- `GET /api/skills/paperclip` returns the Paperclip heartbeat skill markdown.
   459|
   460|## OpenClaw Join Smoke Test
   461|
   462|Run the end-to-end OpenClaw join smoke harness:
   463|
   464|```sh
   465|pnpm smoke:openclaw-join
   466|```
   467|
   468|What it validates:
   469|
   470|- invite creation for agent-only join
   471|- agent join request using `adapterType=openclaw`
   472|- board approval + one-time API key claim semantics
   473|- callback delivery on wakeup to a dockerized OpenClaw-style webhook receiver
   474|
   475|Required permissions:
   476|
   477|- This script performs board-governed actions (create invite, approve join, wakeup another agent).
   478|- In authenticated mode, run with board auth via `PAPERCLIP_AUTH_HEADER` or `PAPERCLIP_COOKIE`.
   479|
   480|Optional auth flags (for authenticated mode):
   481|
   482|- `PAPERCLIP_AUTH_HEADER` (for example `Bearer ...`)
   483|- `PAPERCLIP_COOKIE` (session cookie header value)
   484|
   485|## OpenClaw Docker UI One-Command Script
   486|
   487|To boot OpenClaw in Docker and print a host-browser dashboard URL in one command:
   488|
   489|```sh
   490|pnpm smoke:openclaw-docker-ui
   491|```
   492|
   493|This script lives at `scripts/smoke/openclaw-docker-ui.sh` and automates clone/build/config/start for Compose-based local OpenClaw UI testing.
   494|
   495|Pairing behavior for this smoke script:
   496|
   497|- default `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_DEVICE_AUTH=*** (no Control UI pairing prompt for local smoke; no extra pairing env vars required)
   498|- set `OPENCLAW_DISABLE_DEVICE_AUTH=*** to require standard device pairing
   499|
   500|Model behavior for this smoke script:
   501|
   502|- defaults to OpenAI models (`openai/gpt-5.2` + OpenAI fallback) so it does not require Anthropic auth by default
   503|
   504|State behavior for this smoke script:
   505|
   506|- defaults to isolated config dir `~/.openclaw-paperclip-smoke`
   507|- resets smoke agent state each run by default (`OPENCLAW_RESET_STATE=1`) to avoid stale provider/auth drift
   508|
   509|Networking behavior for this smoke script:
   510|
   511|- auto-detects and prints a Paperclip host URL reachable from inside OpenClaw Docker
   512|- default container-side host alias is `host.docker.internal` (override with `PAPERCLIP_HOST_FROM_CONTAINER` / `PAPERCLIP_HOST_PORT`)
   513|- if Paperclip rejects container hostnames in authenticated/private mode, allow `host.docker.internal` via `pnpm paperclipai allowed-hostname host.docker.internal` and restart Paperclip
   514|", "total_lines": 513, "file_size": 19128, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|# Database
     2|
     3|Paperclip uses PostgreSQL via [Drizzle ORM](https://orm.drizzle.team/). There are three ways to run the database, from simplest to most production-ready.
     4|
     5|## 1. Embedded PostgreSQL — zero config
     6|
     7|If you don't set `DATABASE_URL`, the server automatically starts an embedded PostgreSQL instance and manages a local data directory.
     8|
     9|```sh
    10|pnpm dev
    11|```
    12|
    13|That's it. On first start the server:
    14|
    15|1. Creates a `~/.paperclip/instances/default/db/` directory for storage
    16|2. Ensures the `paperclip` database exists
    17|3. Runs migrations automatically for empty databases
    18|4. Starts serving requests
    19|
    20|Data persists across restarts in `~/.paperclip/instances/default/db/`. To reset local dev data, delete that directory.
    21|
    22|If you need to apply pending migrations manually, run:
    23|
    24|```sh
    25|pnpm db:migrate
    26|```
    27|
    28|When `DATABASE_URL` is unset, this command targets the current embedded PostgreSQL instance for your active Paperclip config/instance.
    29|
    30|This mode is ideal for local development and one-command installs.
    31|
    32|Docker note: the Docker quickstart image also uses embedded PostgreSQL by default. Persist `/paperclip` to keep DB state across container restarts (see `doc/DOCKER.md`).
    33|
    34|## 2. Local PostgreSQL (Docker)
    35|
    36|For a full PostgreSQL server locally, use the included Docker Compose setup:
    37|
    38|```sh
    39|docker compose up -d
    40|```
    41|
    42|This starts PostgreSQL 17 on `localhost:5432`. Then set the connection string:
    43|
    44|```sh
    45|cp .env.example .env
    46|# .env already contains:
    47|# DATABASE_URL=postgres://paperclip:***@localhost:5432/paperclip
    48|```
    49|
    50|Run migrations (once the migration generation issue is fixed) or use `drizzle-kit push`:
    51|
    52|```sh
    53|DATABASE_URL=postgres://paperclip:***@localhost:5432/paperclip \
    54|  npx drizzle-kit push
    55|```
    56|
    57|Start the server:
    58|
    59|```sh
    60|pnpm dev
    61|```
    62|
    63|## 3. Hosted PostgreSQL (Supabase)
    64|
    65|For production, use a hosted PostgreSQL provider. [Supabase](https://supabase.com/) is a good option with a free tier.
    66|
    67|### Setup
    68|
    69|1. Create a project at [database.new](https://database.new)
    70|2. Go to **Project Settings > Database > Connection string**
    71|3. Copy the URI and replace the password placeholder with your database password
    72|
    73|### Connection string
    74|
    75|Supabase offers two connection modes:
    76|
    77|**Direct connection** (port 5432) — use for migrations and one-off scripts:
    78|
    79|```
    80|postgres://postgres.[PROJECT-REF]:***@aws-0-[REGION].pooler.supabase.com:5432/postgres
    81|```
    82|
    83|**Connection pooling via Supavisor** (port 6543) — use for the application:
    84|
    85|```
    86|postgres://postgres.[PROJECT-REF]:***@aws-0-[REGION].pooler.supabase.com:6543/postgres
    87|```
    88|
    89|### Configure
    90|
    91|Set `DATABASE_URL` in your `.env`:
    92|
    93|```sh
    94|DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres.[PROJECT-REF]:***@aws-0-[REGION].pooler.supabase.com:6543/postgres
    95|```
    96|
    97|If using connection pooling (port 6543), the `postgres` client must disable prepared statements. Update `packages/db/src/client.ts`:
    98|
    99|```ts
   100|export function createDb(url: string) {
   101|  const sql = postgres(url, { prepare: false });
   102|  return drizzlePg(sql, { schema });
   103|}
   104|```
   105|
   106|### Push the schema
   107|
   108|```sh
   109|# Use the direct connection (port 5432) for schema changes
   110|DATABASE_URL=postgres://postgres.[PROJECT-REF]:***@...5432/postgres \
   111|  npx drizzle-kit push
   112|```
   113|
   114|### Free tier limits
   115|
   116|- 500 MB database storage
   117|- 200 concurrent connections
   118|- Projects pause after 1 week of inactivity
   119|
   120|See [Supabase pricing](https://supabase.com/pricing) for current details.
   121|
   122|## Switching between modes
   123|
   124|The database mode is controlled by `DATABASE_URL`:
   125|
   126|| `DATABASE_URL` | Mode |
   127||---|---|
   128|| Not set | Embedded PostgreSQL (`~/.paperclip/instances/default/db/`) |
   129|| `postgres://...localhost...` | Local Docker PostgreSQL |
   130|| `postgres://...supabase.com...` | Hosted Supabase |
   131|
   132|Your Drizzle schema (`packages/db/src/schema/`) stays the same regardless of mode.
   133|
   134|## Secret storage
   135|
   136|Paperclip stores secret metadata and versions in:
   137|
   138|- `company_secrets`
   139|- `company_secret_versions`
   140|
   141|For local/default installs, the active provider is `local_encrypted`:
   142|
   143|- Secret material is encrypted at rest with a local master key.
   144|- Default key file: `~/.paperclip/instances/default/secrets/master.key` (auto-created if missing).
   145|- CLI config location: `~/.paperclip/instances/default/config.json` under `secrets.localEncrypted.keyFilePath`.
   146|
   147|Optional overrides:
   148|
   149|- `PAPERCLIP_SECRETS_MASTER_KEY` (32-byte key as base64, hex, or raw 32-char string)
   150|- `PAPERCLIP_SECRETS_MASTER_KEY_FILE` (custom key file path)
   151|
   152|Strict mode to block new inline sensitive env values:
   153|
   154|```sh
   155|PAPERCLIP_SECRETS_STRICT_MODE=***
   156|```
   157|
   158|You can set strict mode and provider defaults via:
   159|
   160|```sh
   161|pnpm paperclipai configure --section secrets
   162|```
   163|
   164|Inline secret migration command:
   165|
   166|```sh
   167|pnpm secrets:migrate-inline-env --apply
   168|```
   169|", "total_lines": 168, "file_size": 4702, "truncated": false, "is_binary": false, "is_image": false}

{"content": "     1|# Paperclip V1 Implementation Spec
     2|
     3|Status: Implementation contract for first release (V1)
     4|Date: 2026-02-17
     5|Audience: Product, engineering, and agent-integration authors
     6|Source inputs: `GOAL.md`, `PRODUCT.md`, `SPEC.md`, `DATABASE.md`, current monorepo code
     7|
     8|## 1. Document Role
     9|
    10|`SPEC.md` remains the long-horizon product spec.
    11|This document is the concrete, build-ready V1 contract.
    12|When there is a conflict, `SPEC-implementation.md` controls V1 behavior.
    13|
    14|## 2. V1 Outcomes
    15|
    16|Paperclip V1 must provide a full control-plane loop for autonomous agents:
    17|
    18|1. A human board creates a company and defines goals.
    19|2. The board creates and manages agents in an org tree.
    20|3. Agents receive and execute tasks via heartbeat invocations.
    21|4. All work is tracked through tasks/comments with audit visibility.
    22|5. Token/cost usage is reported and budget limits can stop work.
    23|6. The board can intervene anywhere (pause agents/tasks, override decisions).
    24|
    25|Success means one operator can run a small AI-native company end-to-end with clear visibility and control.
    26|
    27|## 3. Explicit V1 Product Decisions
    28|
    29|These decisions close open questions from `SPEC.md` for V1.
    30|
    31|| Topic | V1 Decision |
    32||---|---|
    33|| Tenancy | Single-tenant deployment, multi-company data model |
    34|| Company model | Company is first-order; all business entities are company-scoped |
    35|| Board | Single human board operator per deployment |
    36|| Org graph | Strict tree (`reports_to` nullable root); no multi-manager reporting |
    37|| Visibility | Full visibility to board and all agents in same company |
    38|| Communication | Tasks + comments only (no separate chat system) |
    39|| Task ownership | Single assignee; atomic checkout required for `in_progress` transition |
    40|| Recovery | No automatic reassignment; work recovery stays manual/explicit |
    41|| Agent adapters | Built-in `process` and `http` adapters |
    42|| Auth | Mode-dependent human auth (`local_trusted` implicit board in current code; authenticated mode uses sessions), API keys for agents |
    43|| Budget period | Monthly UTC calendar window |
    44|| Budget enforcement | Soft alerts + hard limit auto-pause |
    45|| Deployment modes | Canonical model is `local_trusted` + `authenticated` with `private/public` exposure policy (see `doc/DEPLOYMENT-MODES.md`) |
    46|
    47|## 4. Current Baseline (Repo Snapshot)
    48|
    49|As of 2026-02-17, the repo already includes:
    50|
    51|- Node + TypeScript backend with REST CRUD for `agents`, `projects`, `goals`, `issues`, `activity`
    52|- React UI pages for dashboard/agents/projects/goals/issues lists
    53|- PostgreSQL schema via Drizzle with embedded PostgreSQL fallback when `DATABASE_URL` is unset
    54|
    55|V1 implementation extends this baseline into a company-centric, governance-aware control plane.
    56|
    57|## 5. V1 Scope
    58|
    59|## 5.1 In Scope
    60|
    61|- Company lifecycle (create/list/get/update/archive)
    62|- Goal hierarchy linked to company mission
    63|- Agent lifecycle with org structure and adapter configuration
    64|- Task lifecycle with parent/child hierarchy and comments
    65|- Atomic task checkout and explicit task status transitions
    66|- Board approvals for hires and CEO strategy proposal
    67|- Heartbeat invocation, status tracking, and cancellation
    68|- Cost event ingestion and rollups (agent/task/project/company)
    69|- Budget settings and hard-stop enforcement
    70|- Board web UI for dashboard, org chart, tasks, agents, approvals, costs
    71|- Agent-facing API contract (task read/write, heartbeat report, cost report)
    72|- Auditable activity log for all mutating actions
    73|
    74|## 5.2 Out of Scope (V1)
    75|
    76|- Plugin framework and third-party extension SDK
    77|- Revenue/expense accounting beyond model/token costs
    78|- Knowledge base subsystem
    79|- Public marketplace (ClipHub)
    80|- Multi-board governance or role-based human permission granularity
    81|- Automatic self-healing orchestration (auto-reassign/retry planners)
    82|
    83|## 6. Architecture
    84|
    85|## 6.1 Runtime Components
    86|
    87|- `server/`: REST API, auth, orchestration services
    88|- `ui/`: Board operator interface
    89|- `packages/db/`: Drizzle schema, migrations, DB clients (Postgres)
    90|- `packages/shared/`: Shared API types, validators, constants
    91|
    92|## 6.2 Data Stores
    93|
    94|- Primary: PostgreSQL
    95|- Local default: embedded PostgreSQL at `~/.paperclip/instances/default/db`
    96|- Optional local prod-like: Docker Postgres
    97|- Optional hosted: Supabase/Postgres-compatible
    98|- File/object storage:
    99|  - local default: `~/.paperclip/instances/default/data/storage` (`local_disk`)
   100|  - cloud: S3-compatible object storage (`s3`)
   101|
   102|## 6.3 Background Processing
   103|
   104|A lightweight scheduler/worker in the server process handles:
   105|
   106|- heartbeat trigger checks
   107|- stuck run detection
   108|- budget threshold checks
   109|
   110|Separate queue infrastructure is not required for V1.
   111|
   112|## 7. Canonical Data Model (V1)
   113|
   114|All core tables include `id`, `created_at`, `updated_at` unless noted.
   115|
   116|## 7.0 Auth Tables
   117|
   118|Human auth tables (`users`, `sessions`, and provider-specific auth artifacts) are managed by the selected auth library. This spec treats them as required dependencies and references `users.id` where user attribution is needed.
   119|
   120|## 7.1 `companies`
   121|
   122|- `id` uuid pk
   123|- `name` text not null
   124|- `description` text null
   125|- `status` enum: `active | paused | archived`
   126|
   127|Invariant: every business record belongs to exactly one company.
   128|
   129|## 7.2 `agents`
   130|
   131|- `id` uuid pk
   132|- `company_id` uuid fk `companies.id` not null
   133|- `name` text not null
   134|- `role` text not null
   135|- `title` text null
   136|- `status` enum: `active | paused | idle | running | error | terminated`
   137|- `reports_to` uuid fk `agents.id` null
   138|- `capabilities` text null
   139|- `adapter_type` enum: `process | http`
   140|- `adapter_config` jsonb not null
   141|- `context_mode` enum: `thin | fat` default `thin`
   142|- `budget_monthly_cents` int not null default 0
   143|- `spent_monthly_cents` int not null default 0
   144|- `last_heartbeat_at` timestamptz null
   145|
   146|Invariants:
   147|
   148|- agent and manager must be in same company
   149|- no cycles in reporting tree
   150|- `terminated` agents cannot be resumed
   151|
   152|## 7.3 `agent_api_keys`
   153|
   154|- `id` uuid pk
   155|- `agent_id` uuid fk `agents.id` not null
   156|- `company_id` uuid fk `companies.id` not null
   157|- `name` text not null
   158|- `key_hash` text not null
   159|- `last_used_at` timestamptz null
   160|- `revoked_at` timestamptz null
   161|
   162|Invariant: plaintext key shown once at creation; only hash stored.
   163|
   164|## 7.4 `goals`
   165|
   166|- `id` uuid pk
   167|- `company_id` uuid fk not null
   168|- `title` text not null
   169|- `description` text null
   170|- `level` enum: `company | team | agent | task`
   171|- `parent_id` uuid fk `goals.id` null
   172|- `owner_agent_id` uuid fk `agents.id` null
   173|- `status` enum: `planned | active | achieved | cancelled`
   174|
   175|Invariant: at least one root `company` level goal per company.
   176|
   177|## 7.5 `projects`
   178|
   179|- `id` uuid pk
   180|- `company_id` uuid fk not null
   181|- `goal_id` uuid fk `goals.id` null
   182|- `name` text not null
   183|- `description` text null
   184|- `status` enum: `backlog | planned | in_progress | completed | cancelled`
   185|- `lead_agent_id` uuid fk `agents.id` null
   186|- `target_date` date null
   187|- `env` jsonb null (same secret-aware env binding format used by agent config)
   188|
   189|Invariant:
   190|
   191|- project env is merged into run environment for issues in that project and overrides conflicting agent env keys before Paperclip runtime-owned keys are injected
   192|
   193|## 7.6 `issues` (core task entity)
   194|
   195|- `id` uuid pk
   196|- `company_id` uuid fk not null
   197|- `project_id` uuid fk `projects.id` null
   198|- `goal_id` uuid fk `goals.id` null
   199|- `parent_id` uuid fk `issues.id` null
   200|- `title` text not null
   201|- `description` text null
   202|- `status` enum: `backlog | todo | in_progress | in_review | done | blocked | cancelled`
   203|- `priority` enum: `critical | high | medium | low`
   204|- `assignee_agent_id` uuid fk `agents.id` null
   205|- `created_by_agent_id` uuid fk `agents.id` null
   206|- `created_by_user_id` uuid fk `users.id` null
   207|- `request_depth` int not null default 0
   208|- `billing_code` text null
   209|- `started_at` timestamptz null
   210|- `completed_at` timestamptz null
   211|- `cancelled_at` timestamptz null
   212|
   213|Invariants:
   214|
   215|- single assignee only
   216|- task must trace to company goal chain via `goal_id`, `parent_id`, or project-goal linkage
   217|- `in_progress` requires assignee
   218|- terminal states: `done | cancelled`
   219|
   220|## 7.7 `issue_comments`
   221|
   222|- `id` uuid pk
   223|- `company_id` uuid fk not null
   224|- `issue_id` uuid fk `issues.id` not null
   225|- `author_agent_id` uuid fk `agents.id` null
   226|- `author_user_id` uuid fk `users.id` null
   227|- `body` text not null
   228|
   229|## 7.8 `heartbeat_runs`
   230|
   231|- `id` uuid pk
   232|- `company_id` uuid fk not null
   233|- `agent_id` uuid fk not null
   234|- `invocation_source` enum: `scheduler | manual | callback`
   235|- `status` enum: `queued | running | succeeded | failed | cancelled | timed_out`
   236|- `started_at` timestamptz null
   237|- `finished_at` timestamptz null
   238|- `error` text null
   239|- `external_run_id` text null
   240|- `context_snapshot` jsonb null
   241|
   242|## 7.9 `cost_events`
   243|
   244|- `id` uuid pk
   245|- `company_id` uuid fk not null
   246|- `agent_id` uuid fk `agents.id` not null
   247|- `issue_id` uuid fk `issues.id` null
   248|- `project_id` uuid fk `projects.id` null
   249|- `goal_id` uuid fk `goals.id` null
   250|- `billing_code` text null
   251|- `provider` text not null
   252|- `model` text not null
   253|- `input_tokens` int not null default 0
   254|- `output_tokens` int not null default 0
   255|- `cost_cents` int not null
   256|- `occurred_at` timestamptz not null
   257|
   258|Invariant: each event must attach to agent and company; rollups are aggregation, never manually edited.
   259|
   260|## 7.10 `approvals`
   261|
   262|- `id` uuid pk
   263|- `company_id` uuid fk not null
   264|- `type` enum: `hire_agent | approve_ceo_strategy`
   265|- `requested_by_agent_id` uuid fk `agents.id` null
   266|- `requested_by_user_id` uuid fk `users.id` null
   267|- `status` enum: `pending | approved | rejected | cancelled`
   268|- `payload` jsonb not null
   269|- `decision_note` text null
   270|- `decided_by_user_id` uuid fk `users.id` null
   271|- `decided_at` timestamptz null
   272|
   273|## 7.11 `activity_log`
   274|
   275|- `id` uuid pk
   276|- `company_id` uuid fk not null
   277|- `actor_type` enum: `agent | user | system`
   278|- `actor_id` uuid/text not null
   279|- `action` text not null
   280|- `entity_type` text not null
   281|- `entity_id` uuid/text not null
   282|- `details` jsonb null
   283|- `created_at` timestamptz not null default now()
   284|
   285|## 7.12 `company_secrets` + `company_secret_versions`
   286|
   287|- Secret values are not stored inline in `agents.adapter_config.env`.
   288|- Agent env entries should use secret refs for sensitive values.
   289|- `company_secrets` tracks identity/provider metadata per company.
   290|- `company_secret_versions` stores encrypted/reference material per version.
   291|- Default provider in local deployments: `local_encrypted`.
   292|
   293|Operational policy:
   294|
   295|- Config read APIs redact sensitive plain values.
   296|- Activity and approval payloads must not persist raw sensitive values.
   297|- Config revisions may include redacted placeholders; such revisions are non-restorable for redacted fields.
   298|
   299|## 7.13 Required Indexes
   300|
   301|- `agents(company_id, status)`
   302|- `agents(company_id, reports_to)`
   303|- `issues(company_id, status)`
   304|- `issues(company_id, assignee_agent_id, status)`
   305|- `issues(company_id, parent_id)`
   306|- `issues(company_id, project_id)`

   308|- `cost_events(company_id, agent_id, occurred_at)`

   318|## 7.14 `assets` + `issue_attachments`
   319|
   320|- `assets` stores provider-backed object metadata (not inline bytes):
   321|  - `id` uuid pk
   322|  - `company_id` uuid fk not null
   323|  - `provider` enum/text (`local_disk | s3`)
   324|  - `object_key` text not null
   325|  - `content_type` text not null
   326|  - `byte_size` int not null
   327|  - `sha256` text not null

   329|  - `created_by_agent_id` uuid fk null
   330|  - `created_by_user_id` uuid/text fk null
   331|- `issue_attachments` links assets to issues/comments:
   332|  - `id` uuid pk
   333|  - `company_id` uuid fk not null
   334|  - `issue_id` uuid fk not null
   335|  - `asset_id` uuid fk not null
   336|  - `issue_comment_id` uuid fk null
   337|
   338|## 7.15 `documents` + `document_revisions` + `issue_documents`
   339|
   340|- `documents` stores editable text-first documents:
   341|  - `id` uuid pk
   342|  - `company_id` uuid fk not null
   343|  - `title` text null
   344|  - `format` text not null (`markdown`)
   345|  - `latest_body` text not null
   346|  - `latest_revision_id` uuid null
   347|  - `latest_revision_number` int not null
   348|  - `created_by_agent_id` uuid fk null
   349|  - `created_by_user_id` uuid/text fk null
   350|  - `updated_by_agent_id` uuid fk null
   351|  - `updated_by_user_id` uuid/text fk null
   352|- `document_revisions` stores append-only history:
   353|  - `id` uuid pk
   354|  - `company_id` uuid fk not null
   355|  - `document_id` uuid fk not null
   356|  - `revision_number` int not null
   357|  - `body` text not null
   358|  - `change_summary` text null
   359|- `issue_documents` links documents to issues with a stable workflow key:
   360|  - `id` uuid pk
   361|  - `company_id` uuid fk not null

   363|  - `document_id` uuid fk not null
   364|  - `key` text not null (`plan`, `design`, `notes`, etc.)

   366|## 8. State Machines
   367|
   368|## 8.1 Agent Status
   369|
   370|Allowed transitions:
   371|
   372|- `idle -> running`
   373|- `running -> idle`
   374|- `running -> error`
   375|- `error -> idle`
   376|- `idle -> paused`
   377|- `running -> paused` (requires cancel flow)
   378|- `paused -> idle`
   379|- `* -> terminated` (board only, irreversible)
   380|
   381|## 8.2 Issue Status
   382|
   383|Allowed transitions:
   384|
   385|- `backlog -> todo | cancelled`
   386|- `todo -> in_progress | blocked | cancelled`
   387|- `in_progress -> in_review | blocked | done | cancelled`
   388|- `in_review -> in_progress | done | cancelled`
   389|- `blocked -> todo | in_progress | cancelled`
   390|- terminal: `done`, `cancelled`
   391|
   392|Side effects:
   393|
   394|- entering `in_progress` sets `started_at` if null
   395|- entering `done` sets `completed_at`
   396|- entering `cancelled` sets `cancelled_at`
   397|
   398|## 8.3 Approval Status
   399|
   400|- `pending -> approved | rejected | cancelled`
   401|- terminal after decision
   402|
   403|## 9. Auth and Permissions
   404|
   405|## 9.1 Board Auth
   406|
   407|- Session-based auth for human operator
   408|- Board has full read/write across all companies in deployment
   409|- Every board mutation writes to `activity_log`
   410|
   411|## 9.2 Agent Auth
   412|
   413|- Bearer API key mapped to one agent and company
   414|- Agent key scope:
   415|  - read org/task/company context for own company
   416|  - read/write own assigned tasks and comments
   417|  - create tasks/comments for delegation
   418|  - report heartbeat status
   419|  - report cost events
   420|- Agent cannot:
   421|  - bypass approval gates
   422|  - modify company-wide budgets directly
   423|  - mutate auth/keys
   424|
   425|## 9.3 Permission Matrix (V1)
   426|
   427|| Action | Board | Agent |
   428||---|---|---|
   429|| Create company | yes | no |
   430|| Hire/create agent | yes (direct) | request via approval |
   431|| Pause/resume agent | yes | no |
   432|| Create/update task | yes | yes |
   433|| Force reassign task | yes | limited |
   434|| Approve strategy/hire requests | yes | no |
   435|| Report cost | yes | yes |
   436|| Set company budget | yes | no |
   437|| Set subordinate budget | yes | yes (manager subtree only) |
   438|
   439|## 10. API Contract (REST)
   440|
   441|All endpoints are under `/api` and return JSON.
   442|

   450|- `POST /companies/:companyId/archive`

   461|
   462|- `GET /companies/:companyId/agents`

   472|## 10.4 Tasks (Issues)
   473|
   474|- `GET /companies/:companyId/issues`
   475|- `POST /companies/:companyId/issues`

   483|- `POST /issues/:issueId/checkout`
   484|- `POST /issues/:issueId/release`
   485|- `POST /issues/:issueId/comments`
   486|- `GET /issues/:issueId/comments`
   487|- `POST /companies/:companyId/issues/:issueId/attachments` (multipart upload)

   492|### 10.4.1 Atomic Checkout Contract
   493|
   494|`POST /issues/:issueId/checkout` request:
   495|
   496|```json
   497|{
   498|  "agentId": "uuid",
   499|  "expectedStatuses": ["todo", "backlog", "blocked", "in_review"]
   500|}
   501|```
   502|
   503|Server behavior:
   504|
   505|1. single SQL update with `WHERE id = ? AND status IN (?) AND (assignee_agent_id IS NULL OR assignee_agent_id = :agentId)`
   506|2. if updated row count is 0, return `409` with current owner/status
   507|3. successful checkout sets `assignee_agent_id`, `status = in_progress`, and `started_at`
   508|
   509|## 10.5 Projects
   510|
   511|- `GET /companies/:companyId/projects`
   512|- `POST /companies/:companyId/projects`
   513|- `GET /projects/:projectId`
   514|- `PATCH /projects/:projectId`
   515|
   516|## 10.6 Approvals
   517|
   518|- `GET /companies/:companyId/approvals?status=pending`
   519|- `POST /companies/:companyId/approvals`
   520|- `POST /approvals/:approvalId/approve`
   521|- `POST /approvals/:approvalId/reject`
   522|
   523|## 10.7 Cost and Budgets
   524|
   525|- `POST /companies/:companyId/cost-events`
   526|- `GET /companies/:companyId/costs/summary`
   527|- `GET /companies/:companyId/costs/by-agent`
   528|- `GET /companies/:companyId/costs/by-project`
   529|- `PATCH /companies/:companyId/budgets`
   530|- `PATCH /agents/:agentId/budgets`
   531|
   532|## 10.8 Activity and Dashboard
   533|
   534|- `GET /companies/:companyId/activity`
   535|- `GET /companies/:companyId/dashboard`
   536|
   537|Dashboard payload must include:
   538|
   539|- active/running/paused/error agent counts
   540|- open/in-progress/blocked/done issue counts
   541|- month-to-date spend and budget utilization
   542|- pending approvals count
   543|
   544|## 10.9 Error Semantics
   545|
   546|- `400` validation error
   547|- `401` unauthenticated
   548|- `403` unauthorized
   549|- `404` not found
   550|- `409` state conflict (checkout conflict, invalid transition)
   551|- `422` semantic rule violation
   552|- `500` server error

   554|## 11. Heartbeat and Adapter Contract
   555|
   556|## 11.1 Adapter Interface
   557|
   558|```ts
   559|interface AgentAdapter {
   560|  invoke(agent: Agent, context: InvocationContext): Promise<InvokeResult>;
   561|  status(run: HeartbeatRun): Promise<RunStatus>;
   562|  cancel(run: HeartbeatRun): Promise<void>;
   563|}
   564|```
   565|
   566|## 11.2 Process Adapter
   567|
   568|Config shape:
   569|
   570|```json
   571|{
   572|  "command": "string",
   573|  "args": ["string"],
   574|  "cwd": "string",
   575|  "env": {"KEY": "VALUE"},
   576|  "timeoutSec": 900,
   577|  "graceSec": 15
   578|}
   579|```
   580|
   581|Behavior:
   582|
   583|- spawn child process
   584|- stream stdout/stderr to run logs
   585|- mark run status on exit code/timeout
   586|- cancel sends SIGTERM then SIGKILL after grace
   587|
   588|## 11.3 HTTP Adapter
   589|
   590|Config shape:
   591|
   592|```json
   593|{
   594|  "url": "https://...",
   595|  "method": "POST",
   596|  "headers": {"Authorization": "Bearer ..."},
   597|  "timeoutMs": 15000,
   598|  "payloadTemplate": {"agentId": "{{agent.id}}", "runId": "{{run.id}}"}
   599|}
   600|```
   601|
   602|Behavior:
   603|
   604|- invoke by outbound HTTP request
   605|- 2xx means accepted
   606|- non-2xx marks failed invocation
   607|- optional callback endpoint allows asynchronous completion updates
   608|
   609|## 11.4 Context Delivery
   610|
   611|- `thin`: send IDs and pointers only; agent fetches context via API
   612|- `fat`: include current assignments, goal summary, budget snapshot, and recent comments
   613|
   614|## 11.5 Scheduler Rules
   615|
   616|Per-agent schedule fields in `adapter_config`:
   617|
   618|- `enabled` boolean
   619|- `intervalSec` integer (minimum 30)
   620|- `maxConcurrentRuns` fixed at `1` for V1
   621|
   622|Scheduler must skip invocation when:
   623|
   624|- agent is paused/terminated
   625|- an existing run is active
   626|- hard budget limit has been hit
   627|
   628|## 12. Governance and Approval Flows
   629|
   630|## 12.1 Hiring
   631|
   632|1. Agent or board creates `approval(type=hire_agent, status=pending, payload=agent draft)`.
   633|2. Board approves or rejects.
   634|3. On approval, server creates agent row and initial API key (optional).
   635|4. Decision is logged in `activity_log`.
   636|
   637|Board can bypass request flow and create agents directly via UI; direct create is still logged as a governance action.
   638|
   639|## 12.2 CEO Strategy Approval
   640|
   641|1. CEO posts strategy proposal as `approval(type=approve_ceo_strategy)`.
   642|2. Board reviews payload (plan text, initial structure, high-level tasks).
   643|3. Approval unlocks execution state for CEO-created delegated work.
   644|
   645|Before first strategy approval, CEO may only draft tasks, not transition them to active execution states.
   646|
   647|## 12.3 Board Override
   648|
   649|Board can at any time:
   650|
   651|- pause/resume/terminate any agent
   652|- reassign or cancel any task
   653|- edit budgets and limits
   654|- approve/reject/cancel pending approvals
   655|
   656|## 13. Cost and Budget System
   657|
   658|## 13.1 Budget Layers
   659|
   660|- company monthly budget
   661|- agent monthly budget
   662|- optional project budget (if configured)
   663|
   664|## 13.2 Enforcement Rules
   665|
   666|- soft alert default threshold: 80%
   667|- hard limit: at 100%, trigger:
   668|  - set agent status to `paused`
   669|  - block new checkout/invocation for that agent
   670|  - emit high-priority activity event
   671|
   672|Board may override by raising budget or explicitly resuming agent.
   673|
   674|## 13.3 Cost Event Ingestion
   675|
   676|`POST /companies/:companyId/cost-events` body:
   677|
   678|```json
   679|{
   680|  "agentId": "uuid",
   681|  "issueId": "uuid",
   682|  "provider": "openai",
   683|  "model": "gpt-5",
   684|  "inputTokens": 1234,
   685|  "outputTokens": 567,
   686|  "costCents": 89,
   687|  "occurredAt": "2026-02-17T20:25:00Z",
   688|  "billingCode": "optional"
   689|}
   690|```
   691|
   692|Validation:
   693|
   694|- non-negative token counts
   695|- `costCents >= 0`
   696|- company ownership checks for all linked entities
   697|
   698|## 13.4 Rollups
   699|
   700|Read-time aggregate queries are acceptable for V1.
   701|Materialized rollups can be added later if query latency exceeds targets.
   702|
   703|## 14. UI Requirements (Board App)
   704|
   705|V1 UI routes:
   706|
   707|- `/` dashboard
   708|- `/companies` company list/create
   709|- `/companies/:id/org` org chart and agent status
   710|- `/companies/:id/tasks` task list/kanban
   711|- `/companies/:id/agents/:agentId` agent detail
   712|- `/companies/:id/costs` cost and budget dashboard
   713|- `/companies/:id/approvals` pending/history approvals
   714|- `/companies/:id/activity` audit/event stream
   715|
   716|Required UX behaviors:
   717|
   718|- global company selector
   719|- quick actions: pause/resume agent, create task, approve/reject request
   720|- conflict toasts on atomic checkout failure
   721|- no silent background failures; every failed run visible in UI
   722|
   723|## 15. Operational Requirements
   724|
   725|## 15.1 Environment
   726|
   727|- Node 20+
   728|- `DATABASE_URL` optional
   729|- if unset, auto-use PGlite and push schema
   730|
   731|## 15.2 Migrations
   732|
   733|- Drizzle migrations are source of truth
   734|- no destructive migration in-place for V1 upgrade path
   735|- provide migration script from existing minimal tables to company-scoped schema
   736|
   737|## 15.3 Logging and Audit
   738|
   739|- structured logs (JSON in production)
   740|- request ID per API call
   741|- every mutation writes `activity_log`
   742|
   743|## 15.4 Reliability Targets
   744|
   745|- API p95 latency under 250 ms for standard CRUD at 1k tasks/company
   746|- heartbeat invoke acknowledgement under 2 s for process adapter
   747|- no lost approval decisions (transactional writes)
   748|
   749|## 16. Security Requirements
   750|
   751|- store only hashed agent API keys
   752|- redact secrets in logs (`adapter_config`, auth headers, env vars)
   753|- CSRF protection for board session endpoints
   754|- rate limit auth and key-management endpoints
   755|- strict company boundary checks on every entity fetch/mutation
   756|
   757|## 17. Testing Strategy
   758|
   759|## 17.1 Unit Tests
   760|
   761|- state transition guards (agent, issue, approval)
   762|- budget enforcement rules
   763|- adapter invocation/cancel semantics
   764|
   765|## 17.2 Integration Tests
   766|
   767|- atomic checkout conflict behavior
   768|- approval-to-agent creation flow
   769|- cost ingestion and rollup correctness
   770|- pause while run is active (graceful cancel then force kill)
   771|
   772|## 17.3 End-to-End Tests
   773|
   774|- board creates company -> hires CEO -> approves strategy -> CEO receives work
   775|- agent reports cost -> budget threshold reached -> auto-pause occurs
   776|- task delegation across teams with request depth increment
   777|
   778|## 17.4 Regression Suite Minimum
   779|
   780|A release candidate is blocked unless these pass:
   781|
   782|1. auth boundary tests
   783|2. checkout race test

   826|## 19. Acceptance Criteria (Release Gate)
   827|
   828|V1 is complete only when all criteria are true:
   829|
   830|1. A board user can create multiple companies and switch between them.
   831|2. A company can run at least one active heartbeat-enabled agent.
   832|3. Task checkout is conflict-safe with `409` on concurrent claims.
   833|4. Agents can update tasks/comments and report costs with API keys only.
   834|5. Board can approve/reject hire and CEO strategy requests in UI.
   835|6. Budget hard limit auto-pauses an agent and prevents new invocations.
   836|7. Dashboard shows accurate counts/spend from live DB data.

   840|## 20. Post-V1 Backlog (Explicitly Deferred)

   847|
   848|## 21. Company Portability Package (V1 Addendum)
   849|
   850|V1 supports company import/export using a portable package contract:
   851|
   852|- markdown-first package rooted at `COMPANY.md`
   853|- implicit folder discovery by convention
   854|- `.paperclip.yaml` sidecar for Paperclip-specific fidelity
   855|- canonical base package is vendor-neutral and aligned with `docs/companies/companies-spec.md`
   856|- common conventions:
   857|  - `agents/<slug>/AGENTS.md`
   858|  - `teams/<slug>/TEAM.md`
   859|  - `projects/<slug>/PROJECT.md`
   860|  - `projects/<slug>/tasks/<slug>/TASK.md`
   861|  - `tasks/<slug>/TASK.md`
   862|  - `skills/<slug>/SKILL.md`
   863|
   864|Export/import behavior in V1:
   865|
   866|- export emits a clean vendor-neutral markdown package plus `.paperclip.yaml`
   867|- projects and starter tasks are opt-in export content rather than default package content
   868|- recurring `TASK.md` entries use `recurring: true` in the base package and Paperclip routine fidelity in `.paperclip.yaml`
   869|- Paperclip imports recurring task packages as routines instead of downgrading them to one-time issues
   870|- export strips environment-specific paths (`cwd`, local instruction file paths, inline prompt duplication) while preserving portable project repo/workspace metadata such as `repoUrl`, refs, and workspace-policy references keyed in `.paperclip.yaml`
   871|- export never includes secret values; env inputs are reported as portable declarations instead
   872|- import supports target modes:
   873|  - create a new company
   874|  - import into an existing company
   875|- import recreates exported project workspaces and remaps portable workspace keys back to target-local workspace ids
   876|- import forces imported agent timer heartbeats off so packages never start scheduled runs implicitly
   877|- import supports collision strategies: `rename`, `skip`, `replace`
   878|- import supports preview (dry-run) before apply
   879|- GitHub imports warn on unpinned refs instead of blocking

     3|Target specification for the Paperclip control plane. Living document — updated incrementally during spec interviews.
